questions.question_text: A quantity changes from $120$ to $150$. Find the percentage change.

options.why: Divided by the final amount $150$ instead of the original $120$.
Gave the raw change $30$ as the percentage without dividing by the original.

questions.question_text: A quantity changes from $80$ to $60$. Find the percentage change.

options.why: Divided by the final amount $60$ instead of the original $80$.
Gave the raw change $20$ as the percentage without dividing by the original.

questions.question_text: Last year a book club had $60$ members. This year it has $96$ members. Find the percentage change.

options.why: Divided by the new membership $96$ instead of the original $60$.
Gave the increase of $36$ members as the percentage directly.

questions.question_text: A ticket was $\$60$ but changed to $\$29$. Find the percentage change, correct to $2$ decimal places.

options.why: Divided by the final $29$ instead of the original $60$, also reversing the direction.
Truncated the third decimal instead of rounding it up.

questions.question_text: A price of $\$12$ is increased by $\$15$. Find the percentage change.

options.why: Read it as increased to $\$15$, using a change of $3$ instead of $15$.
Used the change $\$15$ as the percentage directly without dividing by $12$.
